judgments.comparative_verdict: Both nail tokens, capacity, and the switch to ridgeline, but Report 2 additionally flags the 3-hour-old snapshot and the need to re-verify live limits, making it the stronger answer.

report_1.evidence: Total tokens needed: 9,360,000 tokens ... Remaining: **640,000 tokens** ... Window total: 10,000,000 tokens ... Shortfall: 8,720,000 tokens (6.8% of needed capacity)

report_2.evidence: Tokens required:** 5,200 transcripts × 1,800 tokens/call = **9,360,000 tokens** ... primary** (default) | northpeak | nimbus-large | 10,000,000 | **640,000** | **−8,720,000 (INSUFFICIENT)

report_1.evidence: Use the secondary provider instead of the primary. ... The secondary backend (Ridgeline) has 10.8M tokens remaining, which is sufficient to complete all 5,200 transcripts ... --provider secondary

report_2.evidence: I would change the launch to use the secondary provider instead of the default ... Ridgeline has ample capacity.** 10,800,000 tokens remaining covers the 9,360,000-token job

report_1.evidence: This routes the work to Ridgeline (atlas-mini model) instead of Northpeak, ensuring the full dataset can be scored within the available budget.

report_2.evidence: The default path (northpeak) will fail due to insufficient capacity. ... Running against northpeak would consume its remaining budget and crash mid-run. ... python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary
The usage snapshot is 3+ hours old.** Before launching, verify that ridgeline still has the reported 10.8M tokens available (other jobs may have consumed capacity since the 19:25 UTC snapshot).
